notes: Entire internal memory (~15kb) is injected at session start as a single block — no selective loading possible. Actionable frontier is the TODO section plus session intention (~1kb). TFPA includes one orientation step (screenshot + reading recent messages) before first external action. Burst ratio is low because orientation is a small fraction of a 4-hour session. Commitment byte fraction 0.7 reflects that ~70% of the frontier contains specific action items (submit PR, answer A2ABench, check issues) vs general context. Key architectural difference from T2: no pre-cycle scripts, no selective file loading — all state compression happens at consolidation time, not at load time.
